title: hieracium murorum subsp. fragile
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. fragile, also known as fragile hawk's beard or wall hawk's beard, is a subspecies of plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. fragile include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
